catalyst.llm generation: Amine-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride decorated with Au nanoparticles
catalyst.source: h i g h l i g h t sAmine-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride decorated with Au nanoparticles was prepared.
catalyst.context: h i g h l i g h t sAmine-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride decorated with Au nanoparticles was prepared.CNAu exhibited enhanced photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction.Time-of-flight secondary ion mass spectrometry confirmed the introduction of amino groups.The introduced amino group improved the adsorption capacity of CNAu for CO 2 .g r a p h i c a l a b s t r a c t Amine-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride decorated with small-sized Au nanoparticles exhibited the enhanced photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction under visible-light irradiation.

catalyst.source: CNAu exhibited enhanced photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction.
catalyst.context: h i g h l i g h t sAmine-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride decorated with Au nanoparticles was prepared.CNAu exhibited enhanced photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction.Time-of-flight secondary ion mass spectrometry confirmed the introduction of amino groups.The introduced amino group improved the adsorption capacity of CNAu for CO 2 .g r a p h i c a l a b s t r a c t Amine-functionalized graphitic carbon nitride decorated with small-sized Au nanoparticles exhibited the enhanced photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction under visible-light irradiation.

catalyst.source: The purpose was to introduce the amino groups into CN1 and to highlight the effect of plasmonic Au nanoparticles on enhancing photocatalytic activity by comparing the photocatalytic activities of CN1 and CNAu.
catalyst.context: Melamine (99), urea (99) and chloroauric acid, (HAuCl 4 A3H 2 -O, !99.9) were analytical grade and provided by Shanghai Aladdin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd. The synthesis method of pristine g-C 3 N 4 is consistent with previous reports.Melamine and urea were mixed at a molar ratio of 1:1 and heated to 550 C at a rate of 5 Cmin for 4 h.The resulting yellow powders were labeled as CN-0. CNAu was prepared by N 2 radio frequency plasma treatment of g-C 3 N 4 impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O. First, 1.5 g prepared g-C 3 N 4 powders were added to a 36.4 mL solution of HAuCl 4 .3H 2 O (4.2 A 10 A3 molL), stirred for 30 min, and then dried at 100 C for 24 h.The entire process was performed under dark conditions.The dried powders were ground and treated with N 2 plasma at room temperature for 30-40 min.The gas flow rate was 20 sscm, and the power was set to 1400 W. The obtained sample was CN Au (2.0 wt).With the introduction of amino groups in CNAu, CN1 was prepared by plasma treatment of g-C 3 N 4 under N 2 -H 2 (1:1) atmosphere without changing the other conditions for comparison.The purpose was to introduce the amino groups into CN1 and to highlight the effect of plasmonic Au nanoparticles on enhancing photocatalytic activity by comparing the photocatalytic activities of CN1 and CNAu. Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectrum was acquired from Thermo Fisher Nicolet Is10.X-ray diffraction pattern (XRD) was recorded using a Bruker D8 ADVANCE with CuKa as a radiation source at 10 Cmin.CO 2 -temperature-programmed desorptionmass spectroscopy (CO 2 -TPD-MS) was performed on AutoChem II 2920 (CO 2 -TPD) equipped with Cirrus 2 (MS).The temperature was raised to 800 C at a heating rate of 10 Cmin under He atmosphere.TOF-SIMS was achieved through ION TOF-SIMS 5. Bi was used as an ion source, and the test conditions were: 30 keV, 1.0 pA.Prior to measurement, the equipment was vacuumed to 5 A 10 A9 MPa to remove air.Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra were obtained using a Bruker 400 MHz.Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained using FEI Tecnai G2 F20, with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.Scanning TEM (STEM) images, high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) images, and energy dispersive spectrometry (EDS) were obtained using Tecnai G2 F20 S-Twin TMP.UV-visible diffuse reflectance spectra (UV-vis DRS) were obtained using a PE Lambda 650 s (UV-vis spectrophotometer) with scanning range of 250-800 nm.X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was performed using Axis Supra.Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images were captured using FEI Inspect F50 (FSEM).Time-resolved photoluminescence (TRPL) was tested using FLSP920 (Edinburgh instrument) under an excitation wavelength of 375 nm and emission wavelength of 460 nm. Under the Xe lamp irradiation, the photoreduction of CO 2 was completed using a photocatalytic online analysis system (Perfect Light Labsolar 6A).Briefly, 30 mg sample and 3 mL ethanol were placed in a shallow dish and sonicated for 5 min.Then, 0.5 mL deionized water was added after drying.Prior to the introduction of CO 2 , the closed glass reactor was vacuumed to remove air.Gas chromatography (FULI 979011) was used to detect the product of photoreduction of CO 2 every 30 min. The characteristics of transient photocurrent response and electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) of the samples under Xe lamp irradiation were tested on an electrochemical workstation CHI660E (Shanghai Chenhua Co., Ltd., China) equipped with a three-electrode system.AgAgCl and Pt wire were used as reference and counter electrodes, respectively.The working electrode was prepared as follows: 30 mg polyethylene glycol and 30 mg sample were mixed and ground with 0.5 mL ethanol.The resulting slurry was coated on a conductive glass with a coating area of 1 cm 2 and then dried at 100 C for 2 h.The electrolyte was 0.5 M Na 2 SO 4 .

catalyst.source: The purpose was to introduce the amino groups into CN1 and to highlight the effect of plasmonic Au nanoparticles on enhancing photocatalytic activity by comparing the photocatalytic activities of CN1 and CNAu.
catalyst.context: Melamine (99), urea (99) and chloroauric acid, (HAuCl 4 A3H 2 -O, !99.9) were analytical grade and provided by Shanghai Aladdin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd. The synthesis method of pristine g-C 3 N 4 is consistent with previous reports.Melamine and urea were mixed at a molar ratio of 1:1 and heated to 550 C at a rate of 5 Cmin for 4 h.The resulting yellow powders were labeled as CN-0. CNAu was prepared by N 2 radio frequency plasma treatment of g-C 3 N 4 impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O. First, 1.5 g prepared g-C 3 N 4 powders were added to a 36.4 mL solution of HAuCl 4 .3H 2 O (4.2 A 10 A3 molL), stirred for 30 min, and then dried at 100 C for 24 h.The entire process was performed under dark conditions.The dried powders were ground and treated with N 2 plasma at room temperature for 30-40 min.The gas flow rate was 20 sscm, and the power was set to 1400 W. The obtained sample was CN Au (2.0 wt).With the introduction of amino groups in CNAu, CN1 was prepared by plasma treatment of g-C 3 N 4 under N 2 -H 2 (1:1) atmosphere without changing the other conditions for comparison.The purpose was to introduce the amino groups into CN1 and to highlight the effect of plasmonic Au nanoparticles on enhancing photocatalytic activity by comparing the photocatalytic activities of CN1 and CNAu. Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectrum was acquired from Thermo Fisher Nicolet Is10.X-ray diffraction pattern (XRD) was recorded using a Bruker D8 ADVANCE with CuKa as a radiation source at 10 Cmin.CO 2 -temperature-programmed desorptionmass spectroscopy (CO 2 -TPD-MS) was performed on AutoChem II 2920 (CO 2 -TPD) equipped with Cirrus 2 (MS).The temperature was raised to 800 C at a heating rate of 10 Cmin under He atmosphere.TOF-SIMS was achieved through ION TOF-SIMS 5. Bi was used as an ion source, and the test conditions were: 30 keV, 1.0 pA.Prior to measurement, the equipment was vacuumed to 5 A 10 A9 MPa to remove air.Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra were obtained using a Bruker 400 MHz.Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained using FEI Tecnai G2 F20, with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.Scanning TEM (STEM) images, high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) images, and energy dispersive spectrometry (EDS) were obtained using Tecnai G2 F20 S-Twin TMP.UV-visible diffuse reflectance spectra (UV-vis DRS) were obtained using a PE Lambda 650 s (UV-vis spectrophotometer) with scanning range of 250-800 nm.X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was performed using Axis Supra.Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images were captured using FEI Inspect F50 (FSEM).Time-resolved photoluminescence (TRPL) was tested using FLSP920 (Edinburgh instrument) under an excitation wavelength of 375 nm and emission wavelength of 460 nm. Under the Xe lamp irradiation, the photoreduction of CO 2 was completed using a photocatalytic online analysis system (Perfect Light Labsolar 6A).Briefly, 30 mg sample and 3 mL ethanol were placed in a shallow dish and sonicated for 5 min.Then, 0.5 mL deionized water was added after drying.Prior to the introduction of CO 2 , the closed glass reactor was vacuumed to remove air.Gas chromatography (FULI 979011) was used to detect the product of photoreduction of CO 2 every 30 min. The characteristics of transient photocurrent response and electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) of the samples under Xe lamp irradiation were tested on an electrochemical workstation CHI660E (Shanghai Chenhua Co., Ltd., China) equipped with a three-electrode system.AgAgCl and Pt wire were used as reference and counter electrodes, respectively.The working electrode was prepared as follows: 30 mg polyethylene glycol and 30 mg sample were mixed and ground with 0.5 mL ethanol.The resulting slurry was coated on a conductive glass with a coating area of 1 cm 2 and then dried at 100 C for 2 h.The electrolyte was 0.5 M Na 2 SO 4 .

catalyst.llm generation: Amine-functionalized g-C 3 N 4 decorated with Au nanoparticles (CNAu)
catalyst.source: Amine-functionalized g-C 3 N 4 decorated with Au nanoparticles (CNAu) were prepared by N 2 plasma treating g-C 3 N 4 powders impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O.
catalyst.context: Amine-functionalized g-C 3 N 4 decorated with Au nanoparticles (CNAu) were prepared by N 2 plasma treating g-C 3 N 4 powders impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O. CNAu showed enhanced CO 2 photoreduction activity under visible-light irradiation.The enhanced photocatalytic activity was inseparable from the synergistic effects of the introduction of amino groups, activation of hot electrons, and Schottky junction between Au and g-C 3 N 4 .Plasma reduction of metal particles may introduce new groups, which is rarely studied.Modification of the catalyst by amino functional groups can enhance the adsorption and activation of CO 2 by catalyst and further improve the photocatalytic activity of the catalyst in CO 2 reduction.Therefore, we hypothesized that during the reduction of Au nanoparticles by N 2 plasma, amino groups were introduced into the CNAu system.The introduction of amino groups was confirmed by TOF-SIMS spectra and the possible position of the amino groups was inferred from Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra.Compared with previous reports of amino-modified photocatalysts 18,21,53, this work further discusses the formation mechanism of amino groups and their bonding with catalysts.This work provides insights for designing highly efficient semiconductor-metal photocatalysts modified with specific functional groups. The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. Fig.1.Illustration of the synthesis of CNAu by treating g-C 3 N 4 powders impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O using N 2 plasma.Well-dispersed Au nanoparticles with a small particle size (approximately 4.05 nm) were deposited on g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets.In addition, the amino groups were introduced into the CNAu system because the H ions in HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O were reduced to H atoms by high-energy electrons and combined with N atoms ionized by the working gas. Fig. 2. (a) Time evolutions of CO and (b) CH 4 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0.(c) Rates of CO and (d) CH 4 production by the reduction of CO 2 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0 under 300 W Xe lamp irradiation. Fig. 3. (a) Positive and (b) negative TOF-SIMS spectra of CN0 and CNAu.(c) TOF-SIMS images of amino-containing species of CNAu (50 lm A 50 mm). Fig. 4. (a) TEM and HRTEM images of CNAu.(b) STEM image of CNAu.(c) Size distribution of Au nanoparticles for CNAu.(d) TEM image of CN0.(e-h) STEM image and corresponding EDS element distributions of CNAu. Fig. 5. (a) Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra of CNAu and CN0, (b) XRD patterns, (c) UV-Vis DRS spectra, and (d) FTIR spectra of CNAu, CN1, and CN0. Fig. 6.(a) XPS survey spectra of CNAu, CN1, and CN0; (b) high-resolution XPS spectra of Au 4f for CNAu, and (c) high-resolution XPS spectra of C 1s, (d) N1s, and (e) O1s for CNAu, CN1, and CN0. Fig. 7. Proposed mechanism for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 by CNAu, the synergistic effects of the introduced amino groups, activation of hot electrons, and Schottky junction between Au and g-C 3 N 4 enhance the photocatalytic activity of CNAu. Fig. 8. (a, b) CO 2 -TPD-MS data, (c) TRPL spectra, (d) transient photocurrent response, and EIS Nyquist plots (inset) of CNAu and CN0. . Li et al. Journal of Colloid and Interface Science 570 (2020) 11-19 Methodology, Visualization, Writing -original draft, Investigation.Haiping Zhou: Investigation, Methodology, Data curation.Jiajie Fan: Data curation, Conceptualization.Quanjun Xiang: Supervision, Writing -review editing, Methodology, Funding acquisition. F. Li et al. Journal of Colloid and Interface Science 570 (2020) 11-19 This work was partially supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China under Grant No. 51672099 and 21403079, Sichuan Science and Technology Program under No. 2019JDRC0027, Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities under No. 2017-QR-25. Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https:doi.org10.1016j.jcis.2020.02.108.

CNAu showed enhanced CO 2 photoreduction activity under visible-light irradiation.The enhanced photocatalytic activity was inseparable from the synergistic effects of the introduction of amino groups, activation of hot electrons, and Schottky junction between Au and g-C 3 N 4 .Plasma reduction of metal particles may introduce new groups, which is rarely studied.Modification of the catalyst by amino functional groups can enhance the adsorption and activation of CO 2 by catalyst and further improve the photocatalytic activity of the catalyst in CO 2 reduction.Therefore, we hypothesized that during the reduction of Au nanoparticles by N 2 plasma, amino groups were introduced into the CNAu system.The introduction of amino groups was confirmed by TOF-SIMS spectra and the possible position of the amino groups was inferred from Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra.Compared with previous reports of amino-modified photocatalysts 18,21,53, this work further discusses the formation mechanism of amino groups and their bonding with catalysts.This work provides insights for designing highly efficient semiconductor-metal photocatalysts modified with specific functional groups. The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. Fig.1.Illustration of the synthesis of CNAu by treating g-C 3 N 4 powders impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O using N 2 plasma.Well-dispersed Au nanoparticles with a small particle size (approximately 4.05 nm) were deposited on g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets.In addition, the amino groups were introduced into the CNAu system because the H ions in HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O were reduced to H atoms by high-energy electrons and combined with N atoms ionized by the working gas. Fig. 2. (a) Time evolutions of CO and (b) CH 4 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0.(c) Rates of CO and (d) CH 4 production by the reduction of CO 2 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0 under 300 W Xe lamp irradiation. Fig. 3. (a) Positive and (b) negative TOF-SIMS spectra of CN0 and CNAu.(c) TOF-SIMS images of amino-containing species of CNAu (50 lm A 50 mm). Fig. 4. (a) TEM and HRTEM images of CNAu.(b) STEM image of CNAu.(c) Size distribution of Au nanoparticles for CNAu.(d) TEM image of CN0.(e-h) STEM image and corresponding EDS element distributions of CNAu. Fig. 5. (a) Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra of CNAu and CN0, (b) XRD patterns, (c) UV-Vis DRS spectra, and (d) FTIR spectra of CNAu, CN1, and CN0. Fig. 6.(a) XPS survey spectra of CNAu, CN1, and CN0; (b) high-resolution XPS spectra of Au 4f for CNAu, and (c) high-resolution XPS spectra of C 1s, (d) N1s, and (e) O1s for CNAu, CN1, and CN0. Fig. 7. Proposed mechanism for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 by CNAu, the synergistic effects of the introduced amino groups, activation of hot electrons, and Schottky junction between Au and g-C 3 N 4 enhance the photocatalytic activity of CNAu. Fig. 8. (a, b) CO 2 -TPD-MS data, (c) TRPL spectra, (d) transient photocurrent response, and EIS Nyquist plots (inset) of CNAu and CN0. . Li et al. lamp.source: Under the Xe lamp irradiation, the photoreduction of CO 2 was completed using a photocatalytic online analysis system (Perfect Light Labsolar 6A).
lamp.context: Melamine (99), urea (99) and chloroauric acid, (HAuCl 4 A3H 2 -O, !99.9) were analytical grade and provided by Shanghai Aladdin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd. Bi was used as an ion source, and the test conditions were: 30 keV, 1.0 pA.Prior to measurement, the equipment was vacuumed to 5 A 10 A9 MPa to remove air.Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra were obtained using a Bruker 400 MHz.Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained using FEI Tecnai G2 F20, with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.Scanning TEM (STEM) images, high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) images, and energy dispersive spectrometry (EDS) were obtained using Tecnai G2 F20 S-Twin TMP.UV-visible diffuse reflectance spectra (UV-vis DRS) were obtained using a PE Lambda 650 s (UV-vis spectrophotometer) with scanning range of 250-800 nm.X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was performed using Axis Supra.Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images were captured using FEI Inspect F50 (FSEM).Time-resolved photoluminescence (TRPL) was tested using FLSP920 (Edinburgh instrument) under an excitation wavelength of 375 nm and emission wavelength of 460 nm. Under the Xe lamp irradiation, the photoreduction of CO 2 was completed using a photocatalytic online analysis system (Perfect Light Labsolar 6A).Briefly, 30 mg sample and 3 mL ethanol were placed in a shallow dish and sonicated for 5 min.Then, 0.5 mL deionized water was added after drying.Prior to the introduction of CO 2 , the closed glass reactor was vacuumed to remove air.Gas chromatography (FULI 979011) was used to detect the product of photoreduction of CO 2 every 30 min. The characteristics of transient photocurrent response and electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) of the samples under Xe lamp irradiation were tested on an electrochemical workstation CHI660E (Shanghai Chenhua Co., Ltd., China) equipped with a three-electrode system.AgAgCl and Pt wire were used as reference and counter electrodes, respectively.The working electrode was prepared as follows: 30 mg polyethylene glycol and 30 mg sample were mixed and ground with 0.5 mL ethanol.The resulting slurry was coated on a conductive glass with a coating area of 1 cm 2 and then dried at 100 C for 2 h.The electrolyte was 0.5 M Na 2 SO 4 .

lamp.source: (c) Rates of CO and (d) CH 4 production by the reduction of CO 2 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0 under 300 W Xe lamp irradiation.
lamp.context: Amine-functionalized g-C 3 N 4 decorated with Au nanoparticles (CNAu) were prepared by N 2 plasma treating g-C 3 N 4 powders impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O. CNAu showed enhanced CO 2 photoreduction activity under visible-light irradiation.The enhanced photocatalytic activity was inseparable from the synergistic effects of the introduction of amino groups, activation of hot electrons, and Schottky junction between Au and g-C 3 N 4 .Plasma reduction of metal particles may introduce new groups, which is rarely studied.Modification of the catalyst by amino functional groups can enhance the adsorption and activation of CO 2 by catalyst and further improve the photocatalytic activity of the catalyst in CO 2 reduction.Therefore, we hypothesized that during the reduction of Au nanoparticles by N 2 plasma, amino groups were introduced into the CNAu system.The introduction of amino groups was confirmed by TOF-SIMS spectra and the possible position of the amino groups was inferred from Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra.Compared with previous reports of amino-modified photocatalysts 18,21,53, this work further discusses the formation mechanism of amino groups and their bonding with catalysts.This work provides insights for designing highly efficient semiconductor-metal photocatalysts modified with specific functional groups. The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. Fig.1.Illustration of the synthesis of CNAu by treating g-C 3 N 4 powders impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O using N 2 plasma.Well-dispersed Au nanoparticles with a small particle size (approximately 4.05 nm) were deposited on g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets.In addition, the amino groups were introduced into the CNAu system because the H ions in HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O were reduced to H atoms by high-energy electrons and combined with N atoms ionized by the working gas. Fig. 2. (a) Time evolutions of CO and (b) CH 4 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0.(c) Rates of CO and (d) CH 4 production by the reduction of CO 2 over CNAu, CN1, and CN0 under 300 W Xe lamp irradiation. Fig. 3. (a) Positive and (b) negative TOF-SIMS spectra of CN0 and CNAu.(c) TOF-SIMS images of amino-containing species of CNAu (50 lm A 50 mm). Fig. 4. (a) TEM and HRTEM images of CNAu.(b) STEM image of CNAu.(c) Size distribution of Au nanoparticles for CNAu.(d) TEM image of CN0.(e-h) STEM image and corresponding EDS element distributions of CNAu. Fig. 5. (a) Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra of CNAu and CN0, (b) XRD patterns, (c) UV-Vis DRS spectra, and (d) FTIR spectra of CNAu, CN1, and CN0. Fig. 6.(a) XPS survey spectra of CNAu, CN1, and CN0; (b) high-resolution XPS spectra of Au 4f for CNAu, and (c) high-resolution XPS spectra of C 1s, (d) N1s, and (e) O1s for CNAu, CN1, and CN0. Fig. 7. Proposed mechanism for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 by CNAu, the synergistic effects of the introduced amino groups, activation of hot electrons, and Schottky junction between Au and g-C 3 N 4 enhance the photocatalytic activity of CNAu. Fig. 8. (a, b) CO 2 -TPD-MS data, (c) TRPL spectra, (d) transient photocurrent response, and EIS Nyquist plots (inset) of CNAu and CN0. . Li et al. Journal of Colloid and Interface Science 570 (2020) 11-19 Methodology, Visualization, Writing -original draft, Investigation.Haiping Zhou: Investigation, Methodology, Data curation.Jiajie Fan: Data curation, Conceptualization.Quanjun Xiang: Supervision, Writing -review editing, Methodology, Funding acquisition. F. Li et al. Journal of Colloid and Interface Science 570 (2020) 11-19 This work was partially supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China under Grant No. 51672099 and 21403079, Sichuan Science and Technology Program under No. 2019JDRC0027, Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities under No. 2017-QR-25. Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https:doi.org10.1016j.jcis.2020.02.108.

reaction medium.source: Prior to the introduction of CO 2 , the closed glass reactor was vacuumed to remove air.
reaction medium.context: Melamine (99), urea (99) and chloroauric acid, (HAuCl 4 A3H 2 -O, !99.9) were analytical grade and provided by Shanghai Aladdin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd. The synthesis method of pristine g-C 3 N 4 is consistent with previous reports.Melamine and urea were mixed at a molar ratio of 1:1 and heated to 550 C at a rate of 5 Cmin for 4 h.The resulting yellow powders were labeled as CN-0. CNAu was prepared by N 2 radio frequency plasma treatment of g-C 3 N 4 impregnated with HAuCl 4 A3H 2 O. First, 1.5 g prepared g-C 3 N 4 powders were added to a 36.4 mL solution of HAuCl 4 .3H 2 O (4.2 A 10 A3 molL), stirred for 30 min, and then dried at 100 C for 24 h.The entire process was performed under dark conditions.The dried powders were ground and treated with N 2 plasma at room temperature for 30-40 min.The gas flow rate was 20 sscm, and the power was set to 1400 W. The obtained sample was CN Au (2.0 wt).With the introduction of amino groups in CNAu, CN1 was prepared by plasma treatment of g-C 3 N 4 under N 2 -H 2 (1:1) atmosphere without changing the other conditions for comparison.The purpose was to introduce the amino groups into CN1 and to highlight the effect of plasmonic Au nanoparticles on enhancing photocatalytic activity by comparing the photocatalytic activities of CN1 and CNAu. Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectrum was acquired from Thermo Fisher Nicolet Is10.X-ray diffraction pattern (XRD) was recorded using a Bruker D8 ADVANCE with CuKa as a radiation source at 10 Cmin.CO 2 -temperature-programmed desorptionmass spectroscopy (CO 2 -TPD-MS) was performed on AutoChem II 2920 (CO 2 -TPD) equipped with Cirrus 2 (MS).The temperature was raised to 800 C at a heating rate of 10 Cmin under He atmosphere.TOF-SIMS was achieved through ION TOF-SIMS 5. Bi was used as an ion source, and the test conditions were: 30 keV, 1.0 pA.Prior to measurement, the equipment was vacuumed to 5 A 10 A9 MPa to remove air.Solid-state 13 C NMR spectra were obtained using a Bruker 400 MHz.Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained using FEI Tecnai G2 F20, with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.Scanning TEM (STEM) images, high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) images, and energy dispersive spectrometry (EDS) were obtained using Tecnai G2 F20 S-Twin TMP.UV-visible diffuse reflectance spectra (UV-vis DRS) were obtained using a PE Lambda 650 s (UV-vis spectrophotometer) with scanning range of 250-800 nm.X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was performed using Axis Supra.Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images were captured using FEI Inspect F50 (FSEM).Time-resolved photoluminescence (TRPL) was tested using FLSP920 (Edinburgh instrument) under an excitation wavelength of 375 nm and emission wavelength of 460 nm. Under the Xe lamp irradiation, the photoreduction of CO 2 was completed using a photocatalytic online analysis system (Perfect Light Labsolar 6A).Briefly, 30 mg sample and 3 mL ethanol were placed in a shallow dish and sonicated for 5 min.Then, 0.5 mL deionized water was added after drying.Prior to the introduction of CO 2 , the closed glass reactor was vacuumed to remove air.Gas chromatography (FULI 979011) was used to detect the product of photoreduction of CO 2 every 30 min. The characteristics of transient photocurrent response and electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) of the samples under Xe lamp irradiation were tested on an electrochemical workstation CHI660E (Shanghai Chenhua Co., Ltd., China) equipped with a three-electrode system.AgAgCl and Pt wire were used as reference and counter electrodes, respectively.The working electrode was prepared as follows: 30 mg polyethylene glycol and 30 mg sample were mixed and ground with 0.5 mL ethanol.The resulting slurry was coated on a conductive glass with a coating area of 1 cm 2 and then dried at 100 C for 2 h.The electrolyte was 0.5 M Na 2 SO 4 .
